text: After these things, the word of the LORD came to Abram in a vision: fear not, Abram, I am your shield; your reward will be very great.
Abram asked what God could give him, since he remained childless and his heir was Eliezer of Damascus.
Abram said God had given him no offspring, so a servant born in his house would be his heir.
The LORD told him this man would not be his heir; instead, one from his own body would be.
God took him outside and told him to count the stars, if he could; so shall your offspring be.
Abram believed the LORD, and it was counted to him as righteousness.
God said he was the LORD who brought him out of Ur of the Chaldeans to give him this land to possess.
Abram asked how he could know he would possess it.
The LORD told him to bring a heifer, a female goat, a ram, a turtledove, and a young pigeon.
Abram cut the animals in half and arranged the halves opposite each other, but did not cut the birds.
Birds of prey came down on the carcasses, and Abram drove them away.
As the sun was setting, a deep sleep fell on Abram, and a great, dreadful darkness came over him.
The LORD told him his offspring would be sojourners in a land not their own, enslaved and afflicted four hundred years.
God said he would judge the nation they served, and afterward they would come out with great possessions.
Abram himself would go to his fathers in peace, buried at a good old age.
In the fourth generation his offspring would return here, for the sin of the Amorites was not yet complete.
When the sun had set and it was dark, a smoking fire pot and a flaming torch passed between the pieces.
On that day the LORD made a covenant with Abram, giving this land, from the river of Egypt to the Euphrates.
He named the peoples whose land this included: the Kenites, Kenizzites, and Kadmonites.
Also the Hittites, Perizzites, and Rephaim.
And the Amorites, Canaanites, Girgashites, and Jebusites.
